subject: 🏃 Monday — Lower A + Upper Push (Week 1 begins)
html:
Welcome to Day 1 of the 15-week build to SF Marathon 2026. Race day is 104 days out. Today is gym only — no running — which is exactly what your knee needs after yesterday's unplanned 10.5 miles in Cambria (Yellow: tingly and tender afterward).

Today's session: Lower A + Upper Push, ~55 min. Lower body first — it's the priority. 3 rounds of the main circuit, 3 sets of the wall-sit finisher. Given yesterday's Yellow knee, all lower body work stays at bodyweight today except single-leg RDLs, which you can try at 15 lbs/hand only if the knee feels quiet this morning. If there's any awareness, drop RDLs to bodyweight too and we'll load them Friday.

Focus cue: Slow eccentrics. Every downward phase is a controlled 3 seconds — especially the single-leg squat to bench and the step-downs. These two exercises are the #1 prevention tool for runner's knee. Teach the VMO and the knee that this cycle will be controlled.

Where you are: Week 1 of 15, Base phase. The goal of the first two weeks is not fitness — it's confirming the knee tolerates consistent load before tempo work starts Week 3. The Foster City 5K benchmark is 19 days out and will recalibrate your marathon pace targets. Easy 5 mi tomorrow at conversational effort (HR under 140, roughly 9:15–10:00/mi).

Recommended Weights

ExerciseWeightTarget feel
Lower A (all bodyweight today — knee was Yellow)
Single-leg squat to benchBodyweightForm focus — 3-sec descent
Single-leg RDL15 lbs/hand (or BW if knee is noisy)Good (2–3 RIR)
Step-downsBodyweightForm focus — 3-sec descent
Copenhagen plankBodyweight20 sec each side
Calf raisesBodyweightFull ROM, slow lowering
Wall sit (finisher x3)Bodyweight30–45 sec
Upper Push
Dumbbell bench press30 lbs/handGood (2–3 RIR)
Overhead press20 lbs/handGood
Push-upsBodyweight x 12–15Good
Lateral raise10 lbsGood
Tricep overhead extension20 lbsGood

Report back after the session with a feel rating per exercise and your current body weight so we can calibrate Friday's Lower B. Let's go.
